zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde De Saint Clair於1120年出生在法國巴-諾門迪奧內沙吉可萊-德-哈盧澤時，她的父親Guillaume de Saint-Clair25歲，母親Adeline de Montfort26歲。她於1145年與Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1175年享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde De Saint Clair于1120年出生在法国巴-諾門迪奧內沙吉可萊-德-哈盧澤时，她的父亲Guillaume de Saint-Clair25岁，母亲Adeline de Montfort26岁。她于1145年与Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1175年享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Clair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the male personal name Clair (from Latin Clarus ‘illustrious’), which was borne by two early Christian saints, a 3rd-century bishop of Nantes and the 4th-century St. Clair of Aquitaine.
French: nickname for a cheerful individual, from the adjective clair ‘bright, light’. Compare Leclair 1.
American shortened form of French   Leclair   1, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Leclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Emile, Normand, Gaston, Adrien, Antoine, Lucien, Monique, Philias, Camille.

French: nickname for a cheerful individual, a variant of Clair 2 ‘bright, light’, with fused masculine definite article le. Derivation from the male personal name Clair 1 is also possible. Compare Laclair , Laclaire , and Leclear .
French: altered form of   Leclerc   1.

variants.name: Klar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ezriel, Tzvi, Baruch, Devorah, Isadore, Nachman, Yitzchok.  German Gerhard, Johann, Erhard, Kurt, Willibald.
German: nickname from Middle High German and Middle Low German klār ‘pure, beautiful, clear’.
German: from the female personal name Klara, an equivalent of Clara (see Clair and Sinclair ).

variants.name: Cler
meanings: French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Clarin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luz, Alejandro, Armida, Epifano, Florencio, Jose, Libia, Librada, Maria Luisa, Raul, Santos, Sergio.

Swedish: variant of Klarin, ornamental or perhaps habitational name formed with klar ‘clear’ (possibly representing an element of a placename) + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Clair  .

variants.name: Auclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Raoul, Andre, Fernand, Ovila, Alphonse, Cecile, Gaston, Germaine, Jacques, Jean-Paul.
French: patronymic from Clair , from (fils) au Clair ‘(son) of Clair’. Compare Leclerc 2 and Oclair .

History: Pierre and André Auclair, two brothers from Saint-Vivien near La Rochelle in France, are recorded in Charlesbourg, QC, in 1679 and 1681, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Leclerc, Oclair, and O'Clair.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Mechtild
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mechtilde
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde De Saint Clair was born in 1120, in Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Lower Normandy, France, her father, Guillaume de Saint-Clair, was 25 and her mother, Adeline de Montfort, was 26. She married Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er in 1145. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1175, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde De Saint Clair est née en 1120, à Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Guillaume de Saint-Clair, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Adeline de Montfort, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er en 1145. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1175, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde De Saint Clair 1120 in Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume de Saint-Clair 25 und ihre Mutter Adeline de Montfort 26. Sie hat 1145 Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1175 im Alter von 55 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde De Saint Clair è nata il nel 1120 nel Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Guillaume de Saint-Clair, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Adeline de Montfort, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er nel 1145. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1175, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Saint Clairが1120年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンサイント-クレイル-デ-ハロウズで生まれた時，父Guillaume de Saint-Clairは25歳，母Adeline de Montfortは26歳であった。1145年にBaron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1erと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1175年に55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Saint Clair(이)가 1120년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 세인트-c레이-데-할우즈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume de Saint-Clair(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adeline de Montfort(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1145년 Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1175년 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde De Saint Clair nasceu em 1120, em Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Guillaume de Saint-Clair, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Adeline de Montfort, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er em 1145. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1175, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde De Saint Clair родилась в 1120 г. в местности сеинт-клеир-де-хеилоуз, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Saint-Clair было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Adeline de Montfort, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er в 1145 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1175 г. в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde De Saint Clair nació en 1120, en Saint-Clair-de-Halouze, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Guillaume de Saint-Clair, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Adeline de Montfort, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Baron Richard De Creully De Gloucester 1er en 1145. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1175, a la edad de 55 años.
